edges.description: Accumulated cholestanol in neuronal membranes is modeled as an upstream metabolic driver of the peripheral neuropathy branch observed in CTX.

Axonal injury and demyelinating or axonal neuropathy bridge the metabolic lesion to peripheral nerve manifestations.

edges.description: Multisystemic cholestanol deposition includes the eye and contributes to early cataract disease.

Sterol accumulation in tendons causes xanthomatous enlargement.
Achilles tendon involvement is a characteristic tendon xanthoma location.
Bone involvement and surveillance-relevant bone-density loss are captured as osteoporosis.

Orphanet records frequent plantar skin involvement, but the intermediate mechanism is unresolved.

evidence.snippet: Inhibitors of 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A (HMG-CoA) reductase alone or in combination with CDCA are also effective in decreasing cholestanol concentration and improving clinical signs; however, they may induce muscle damage.
